factors.summary: Demand is decelerating due to lower freight rail volumes and increased locomotive parking.

evidence.text: The decrease is primarily attributable to a decrease of $95 million in the Freight Segment due to lower Services sales driven by lower freight rail volumes and an increase in parking of locomotives, particularly in North America and lower sales in Components due to a reduction of freight carbuilds in the third quarter of 2020, which were partially offset by growth in locomotive Equipment sales. The lower freight rail volumes and increased parking of locomotives are primarily due to the COVID-19 pandemic and its impact on the economy.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Demand is decelerating due to lower freight rail volumes and increased locomotive parking, driven by the COVID-19 pandemic.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating due to lower sales in Components and Services.

evidence.text: The decrease is primarily due to an organic decrease of $506 million in the Freight Segment, due to lower sales in Components from a reduction of freight carbuilds in 2020 compared to 2019, a decrease in Services sales due to lower freight rail volumes and an increase in parking of locomotives, particularly in North America, and lower locomotive Equipment sales.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is decelerating due to lower sales in Components and Services, impacted by the COVID-19 pandemic.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are declining, particularly in freight rail and transit segments.

evidence.text: The lower freight rail volumes and increased parking of locomotives are primarily due to the COVID-19 pandemic. The Transit Segment experienced a decrease in sales of $41 million, primarily due to COVID-19 related production delays and reduced passenger traffic.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are declining in freight rail and transit segments, primarily due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

factors.summary: End market momentum is weak, particularly in the Transit Segment due to COVID-19 impacts.

evidence.text: The Transit Segment experienced a decrease in sales of $41 million, primarily due to COVID-19 related production delays and reduced passenger traffic.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: End market momentum is weak, particularly in the Transit Segment, due to COVID-19 related disruptions.

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable with cost-saving measures in place.

evidence.text: Engineering expense decreased $22 million due to cost savings measures put in place in response to the COVID-19 pandemic on research and development projects and amortization expense decreased $9 million.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are manageable with cost-saving measures in place, leading to expense reductions.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are under pressure due to unfavorable sales mix and lower absorption of overhead costs.

evidence.text: The increase can be attributed to an unfavorable sales mix and lower absorption of overhead costs due to the decrease in sales volumes primarily caused by the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic partially offset by increased synergy savings related to the GE Transportation acquisition.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Gross and operating margins are under pressure due to unfavorable sales mix and lower overhead absorption, impacted by the pandemic.

factors.summary: Restructuring and cost reduction programs are delivering measurable savings.

evidence.text: Restructuring costs included in Selling, General, and Administrative expenses ('SG&A') were $12 million and $40 million in the third quarters of 2020 and 2019, respectively. Additionally, SG&A expenses decreased $12 million related to acquisition synergy savings and cost reduction initiatives.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Restructuring and cost reduction programs are delivering measurable savings, evidenced by significant expense decreases.

factors.summary: Operational efficiency is improving due to cost-saving measures.

evidence.text: Excluding these costs in both years, cost of sales as a percentage of sales was 69.4% in 2020 and 67.8% in 2019, representing a 1.6% increase. The increase can be attributed to an unfavorable sales mix and lower absorption of overhead costs due to the decrease in sales volumes primarily caused by the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic partially offset by increased synergy savings related to the GE Transportation acquisition.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Operational efficiency is improving due to cost-saving measures, leading to slight increases in cost as a percentage of sales.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include the ongoing impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on sales and operations.

evidence.text: Results of operations were negatively impacted during the nine months ended September 30, 2020 as a result of the COVID-19 pandemic. Management’s discussion below includes analysis as to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic where it could be explicitly identified; however, in many instances it is difficult to quantify with a high level of certainty the negative impact the COVID-19 pandemic had on our net sales, cost of goods, operating expenses, interest expense, and income tax expense.
evidence.subsection: FIRST NINE MONTHS OF 2020 COMPARED TO FIRST NINE MONTHS OF 2019

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The COVID-19 pandemic has caused significant negative impacts on operations and sales.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are a key sensitivity impacting net sales.

evidence.text: Unfavorable changes in foreign currency exchange rates reduced net sales by $77 million.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Unfavorable foreign currency exchange rates reduced net sales by $77 million.

factors.summary: Headcount restructuring is occurring as part of integration actions and cost reductions.

evidence.text: The third quarter of 2020 includes $5 million of restructuring costs, primarily for the exit of certain product lines, footprint rationalization, and related headcount reductions as part of the ongoing integration actions related to the GE Transportation acquisition and in response to the COVID-19 pandemic.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Restructuring includes headcount reductions due to integration actions and cost reductions.

factors.summary: Labor relations are stable with no notable issues.

evidence.text: Labor relations are stable, improving, or deteriorating? Is there active or potential strike risk, contract disputes, or union pressure?

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: No notable issues or specific sentiment mentioned regarding labor relations.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are acting as a headwind due to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic.

evidence.text: The lower freight rail volumes and increased parking of locomotives are primarily due to the COVID-19 pandemic and its impact on the economy.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Economic conditions are a headwind due to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are impacting net sales unfavorably.

evidence.text: Unfavorable changes in foreign currency exchange rates reduced net sales by $77 million.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Unfavorable foreign currency exchange rates reduced net sales by $77 million.
